keyPoints: The Byzantine Generals Problem is a problem in distributed systems that describes the challenges of achieving consensus among a group of entities.
The CAP theorem states that it is impossible for a distributed data storage system to simultaneously guarantee all three of the following: consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.
Blockchain technology was first proposed by a computer scientist named Stuart Haber and a cryptographer named W. Scott Stornetta in 1989.
The concept of blockchain is based on a decentralized, distributed ledger that records transactions across a network of computers.
The key characteristics of a blockchain system include immutability, transparency, and security.
